div.div: The threadprocs spawned with clone3 and trampolined into the respective ld.so code mappings have been set up to have fd 0,1,2, cwd, signals, etc. match the corresponding launcher process. This threadproc and its decendents are distinct/detached from the server in most respects, EXCEPT that they share the same virtual memory space.

div.div: printstr thread stacks, global state, heap

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 148px; height: 1px; padding-top: 195px; margin-left: 402px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Every threadproc inherits the same virtual memory address space from the server process. Unlike `fork()`, this is not a COW duplicate, but actually the same memory.
I've drawn this as if the memory for each threadproc is contiguous, but it just depends on what `mmap()` decides to provide when called.

div.div: Address 0xcafef00d has stable meaning to the server and all threadprocs, and can be derefenced natively
